review_title: Thank god for redirect.pizza (they got us out of a big jam)
description: There are few times in life that any service (not just a tech service) just works. We were very grateful to solve what was kind of a big deal.
author: Tom C.
pros: Our nonprofit had a problem with link management. Our hosting provider, NationBuilder CMS, provides the www version but if a visitor goes to the non-www version the get a warning message. Nationbuilder sent us to redirect.pizza to solve the problem, and it worked.
cons: There’s was nothing to dislike. The service works, and works well, and the whole thing was quick and easy. A+
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Essential if you are using a registrar without effective redirection.
description: It was very good and smooth. I thought there would be a learning curve, but it's actually immediate.
author: Luiz A.
pros: It's very easy, practical and quick to use. In less than 5m I managed to get around the problem of the root url of my site not working without the www.
cons: For now, nothing to complain about it. I’d like it to be native to my registrar and have redirect.pizza directly on it to be honest.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: It is a fun interface and seemed pretty easy to use, especially if you have more knowledge than I do about website stuff.
author: Macy C.
pros: It was easy to use with the step by step directions provided by ChurchTrac. It was good to have an easy tool to make our website work.
cons: I just wasn't wanting to create another account to make our website work, but that's not redirect.pizza's fault.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Exceptional. Redirect.pizza eliminated hours of frustration caused by registrar and hosting limitations and replaced it with a fast, elegant solution. Setup was straightforward, redirects propagated quickly, and everything behaved exactly as expected from an SEO and UX perspective. It even resolved secondary issues I didn’t initially realize were related. This is now my go-to recommendation for anyone who needs reliable URL forwarding without unnecessary complexity.
author: Sophia P.
pros: Ridiculously easy to set up and it just works. Clean 301 redirects in minutes with zero hosting, zero bloat, and no unnecessary features getting in the way. The interface is intuitive, the documentation is clear, and it solved multiple real-world domain and email-related issues instantly. This is one of those rare tools that does exactly what it promises, quickly and reliably.
cons: Honestly, very little. If you’re looking for a full DNS or hosting platform, this isn’t that, but that’s not a flaw. It’s intentionally focused, and it excels at that focus. The only “con” is wishing I’d found it sooner.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: I found the service while frustrated by SSL errors or "naked" domain issues (like example.com not going to ://example.com). They are usually relieved to find a fix that works in under 5 minutes. Reliability: Once configured, users rarely have to log back in to fix broken links or renew certificates. Developer-Friendly: While simple for beginners, pros appreciate the API and bulk management tools for handling dozens of domains at once.
author: Nicholas K.
pros: Dead Simple Setup: You can fix complex domain issues in under 5 minutes with an interface that has zero learning curve. Automatic SSL/HTTPS: It handles security certificates automatically, so visitors never see those "Your connection is not private" warnings. Fixes "Naked" Domains: It’s the go-to solution for platforms (like Google Sites) that don't natively support redirecting a root domain (example.com) to a www address.
cons: Limited Free Analytics: The free plan lacks advanced tracking features, leaving users in the dark about detailed traffic sources. Restrictive Free Tiers: The limited number of redirects (typically two) and the exclusion of "www" redirects from the root domain count on the free plan to be a hurdle for small multi-domain projects. English-Only Interface: There is currently no multilingual support, which can make it less intuitive for non-English speakers or beginners. Propagation Delays: Although not a fault of the tool itself, I found the DNS update time frustrating when trying to get redirects live instantly.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Definitely easy as eating a pizza and useful
description: My experience ao far has been excellent. It met my needs perfectly for the tier i am in. My website has been stable and redirecting properly.
author: Ezekiel I.
pros: The ease at which i was able link my urls, and automated domain link management. So easy. You really do not have to do much and it has been so reliable.
cons: Nothing so far. Everything works just fine, and i am not sure anything needs to improve. The tiers are also perfectly priced.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Simple, Reliable, and Professional Redirect Solution
description: My overall experience with redirect.pizza has been very positive. The platform is user friendly, quick to set up, and reliable, which makes domain management stress free. It delivers exactly what it promises without unnecessary complications. For freelancers and small businesses, it provides an efficient solution to handle redirects professionally while keeping everything simple and secure.
author: Jose P.
pros: What I liked most about redirect.pizza is how simple and intuitive it is to use compared to other redirect solutions. The platform removes all the technical complexity of DNS management and domain forwarding, making it possible to set up professional redirects in just a few minutes. I also appreciate that it is reliable and transparent: the service works consistently without downtime, and the interface makes it easy to monitor and adjust settings at any time. The fact that it provides a free plan while still keeping the quality high is also a major advantage for small businesses or freelancers.
cons: What I liked least about redirect.pizza is that some advanced features, like detailed analytics or SSL customization, are only available on paid plans. It would be great if the free plan included a bit more flexibility.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Simple, Fast, and Works Every Time
description: My experience’s been great so far. It’s reliable, fast, and does exactly what it promises. I’ve used a couple of other redirect tools before, but this one just feels easier and more polished. Customer support’s also been really helpful when I had questions.
author: Grace A.
pros: Honestly, I love how easy Redirect.pizza is to use. You don’t need to be technical at all, you just set up a redirect, and it works right away. The dashboard is super clean, and everything feels really smooth. It saves me a ton of time.
cons: I wish there were a few more options for tracking and analytics. Like being able to see where traffic is coming from or how each redirect performs over time. Not a dealbreaker though — just something that would make it even better
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

description: I set up a Google Site for a client and attached it to a custom domain. Google apparently makes this a huge pain, including so that SSL certificates take several hours to load and that it's not allowed to use a bare (e.g. example.com) URL, only a subdomain. Redirect Pizza let me redirect the bare to the www, and in the meantime before the SSL kicked in it let me redirect the www to the Google Sites URL, so I didn't have to wait for Sites to come up with the new SSL cert.
author: Alexa G.
pros: It does exactly what it says on the tin, and does it well! No additional latency, and it told me exactly what to put in my DNS listings.
cons: I can't say that I have any real complaints. The free tier was exactly what I needed, and I think it makes sense to pay for it above that.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

description: I recently bought a professional custom domain to redirect recruiters to my ArtStation portfolio. But I needed a redirect service, as my domain provider couldn't handle doing secure redirections, and ArtStation force a subscription fee to use a custom domain. So I found redirect.pizza, which was easy to setup with my domain. It started working quite quickly after I configured the domain. The free tier is quite generous, and fits my needs. Will definitely be sticking with them, and recommending them to my friends who are in a similar situation.
author: Bailey W.
pros: Very simple to setup with my domain. Started working with my domain extremely quick after changing the domain DNS on my domain provider's site. Free plan has the necessities.
cons: Analytics are locked behind a paywall. I don't need in-depth analytics (like location, browsers, etc), but would be nice to at least see how many redirects it has done.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Solves a long-standing Google Sites domain issue
description: The interface is extremely easy to use, and the free plan is generous and sufficient for individuals and small businesses.
author: Jacob M.
pros: This product solved a long-standing problem of Google Sites only providing HTTP forwarding for "naked" domains, but not HTTPS forwarding.
cons: Why did it take me so long to learn about this? I am happy with the free plan as solving my immediate problems. I'm not sure what actual value I would gain from a paid plan, apart from unlimited sites.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months
